summary: End-to-end testing of CSV import + export + template flows on /datasets (backend BFF + React UI). 15/15 backend pytests pass and all 16 frontend Playwright steps pass — including multi-type export (one download per dataset_type).

minor.issue: Returns 500 instead of 404 when the requested instance_id does not exist. Root cause: upstream harness returns 500 for unknown instances; BFF only special-cases r.status_code == 404 (server.py L822). Consider catching upstream 500/422 and mapping to 404 when the URL clearly targets a single instance, OR raise a clean 400/404 with a 'not found' message so the UI doesn't show a generic crash.

action_items: (Optional) Map upstream 500 on /datasets/export → 404 in BFF when a non-existent instance_id is requested. Currently the export catches only 404 (server.py L822) so a typo'd instance_id surfaces as a server error in the UI.
critical_code_review_comments: DatasetsPage.js L274-296: The Tooltip-around-disabled-Button pattern uses a span wrapper with tabIndex toggling — works, but the disabled <button> won't fire pointer events so the tooltip 'Select one or more rows to export' only shows on keyboard focus. Consider rendering a non-disabled button styled as disabled (pointer-events:none on the inner content) so users discover why it's greyed out on hover.

DatasetsPage.js handleExport (L216-240): Sequential `await` inside a `for` loop blocks one type after another. For 2-type exports that's fine, but if a user picks rows across many types this serializes downloads. Consider Promise.allSettled with per-type error toasts.

ImportCsvDialog L142-177: After a successful upload the dialog stays open and the file list stays populated; the user can accidentally hit Upload again (re-importing the same files → 'skipped: N'). Idempotent so no harm done, but consider clearing `files` (not the `result` banner) after a successful import.

ImportCsvDialog L276: Renders `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` for legend callouts. The strings are static (defined in COLUMN_HELP above), but if anyone wires this through user input later, this becomes an XSS hole. Add a comment or wrap the regex output in a small sanitiser.

evalApi.js exportDatasetsCsv (L290-301): Filename parse only handles `filename="…"` and `filename=…`. RFC 5987 filename*=UTF-8'' is not handled. Backend currently emits ASCII so this is fine, but worth a note.

server.py export_datasets_csv (L815-841): Sequential awaits in `for iid in instance_id` — 1 round-trip per instance. For large multi-select (e.g. 50 rows) that's 50 sequential RTTs to the harness. asyncio.gather would parallelise.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_dataset_csv.py (new — covers template, import, re-import skip, bad-row error, unknown type, export by instance_ids, CRUD regression)
seed_data_creation: Imported transient bug_bench + scratch_bench_phased rows with prefixes pw_csv_<ts>_<i> and pw_multi_<ts>_<s|b>. Rows persist (idempotent import covered by test_02). No cleanup performed — instance_ids are uniqued per run with Date.now().
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/test_dataset_csv.py are regression-safe (Date.now-based prefixes). The export-unknown-instance test accepts 404 OR 500 because the upstream harness returns 500 for missing instance lookups — that's the one open BFF quirk worth a future fix. All other CSV flows (template per type, import/skip/bad row, export single + multi-type, select-all, auto-type-switch, cancel) are fully verified end-to-end through the public URL.
rca of the issue: n/a — no functional bugs found. Single minor enhancement opportunity: BFF should normalise upstream 500 on /datasets/export single-instance lookup → 404.
